meta.title: DREAM — Dimensional Resonant Emergent Attractors in Manifold
meta.footer: prototype; core science and speculation are clearly separated.

hero.title: Dimensional Resonant Emergent Attractors in Manifold
hero.subtitle: Our 4D world is a finite‑resolution projection of a compact, atemporal 10D reality. Physics measures invariant shadows; the projection kernel’s parameters are axioms.
hero.note: Toggle Math to reveal equations. Toggle Speculative to show interpretation-only sections.

core.title: Core idea (plain)
core.text: 10→4 projection by a fixed kernel compresses structure; what survives are projection invariants (organization, conserved classes). Constants we measure are kernel‑fixed in our slice.

glance.title: At a glance
glance.item1: Kernel invariants: resolution λq, locality radius, regularity class, spectral exponent.
glance.item2: Two regimes: below λq (quantum‑grade invariants); above (classical coarse‑grain).
glance.item3: Falsifiable forecasts: ALP mass ladder, coherence‑vs‑scale “cliff”, low‑freq torsion tones.

retention.title: Retention law
retention.note: Retained structure decays rapidly once probe resolution exceeds the kernel scale.

abstract.title: Abstract
abstract.plain:
DREAM treats our 4-dimensional world as a finite-resolution projection of a compact, atemporal 10-dimensional Meta-Manifold (MM). What we call “observables” in 4D are push-forwards through a fixed, non-invertible projection kernel. Because the kernel has finite resolution, microscopic detail is averaged out; what systematically survives in 4D are projection invariants (organization, conserved classes, robust spectra). In this posture, the usual constants of nature are not derived—they are kernel-fixed parameters of our particular slice.

The framework is anchored by a few axioms: (1) the MM exists and is smooth and compact; (2) a single, fixed projection kernel maps 10→4 at finite resolution; (3) there is no required U4×K6 split—all 10 coordinates contribute to 4D observables; (4) constants like c, ℏ, G are projection-fixed in our slice; (5) stable topological data of the MM label particle/gauge classes; and (6) finite resolution implies two operational regimes—below the kernel scale we observe “quantum-grade” invariants, above it we recover classical coarse-grain.

Since the kernel is not observed directly, science attaches to kernel invariants—quantities that do not depend on the detailed choice of kernel within a consistent class. The key invariants used operationally are: a resolution scale λq, a locality radius (how sharply the kernel ties MM points to 4D points), regularity/positivity (well-posedness as an operator), and an effective spectral exponent D_eff that governs mode counting. These four control how structure in 10D is retained or erased when seen in 4D.

A central consequence is a retention law: as the effective probe scale λ gets larger than λq, fidelity to fine structure falls off monotonically; below λq fine structure retains. This yields two regimes and a coherence “cliff” around λ≈λq: experiments that scan across that boundary should see a sharp change in interference/coherence observables. In practice, this puts the emphasis on where information survives rather than what microscopic details look like.

From these invariants follow crisp, testable phenomenology. One repeated thread is a non-linear mass/frequency ladder for a light axion-like mode (ALP) with a base around ~65 neV (fundamental frequency ~15.7 MHz) and ratios controlled by D_eff. Another is possible low-frequency torsion/extra polarization content in the mHz band (e.g., LISA scale) tied to large energy hubs. On the lab side, the coherence vs scale transition near the pm regime is a direct probe of λq. Each of these is formulated so an instrument can aim at a concrete window rather than an open-ended search.

Finally, the posture is explicitly falsifiable. If the ALP window at the stated sensitivity is decisively excluded, or if controlled platforms fail to see a retention transition near the posited λq, or if gravitational/torsion anomalies are null at the modeled levels, then the corresponding claims fail. The kernel-invariant stance prevents “saving” the idea by unconstrained kernel tuning: the point is to keep the invariants fixed and let the data decide.
abstract.precise:
Observables as push-forwards. 4D observables are modeled as push-forwards of MM fields by a fixed kernel \(K_\lambda\) (finite resolution \(\lambda\)), i.e. a non-invertible map from MM fields \(\Phi\) to 4D fields \(O\):
\[ O(x) = \int_{\mathrm{MM}} K_\lambda\big(x; X\big) \, \Phi(X) \, d\mu_{\mathrm{MM}}(X),\quad \lambda = \lambda_q \ \text{(our slice)}. \]
Only invariants of \(K_\lambda\) matter empirically; the detailed choice within a well-posed class is not identified by data. Kernel guardrails (well-posedness). We constrain the kernel by minimal conditions:
\[\textbf{K1 (normalization):}\ \int K_\lambda(x;X)\,d\mu_{\mathrm{MM}}(X) < \infty.\]
\[\textbf{K2 (locality in projection):}\ K_\lambda(x;X) \text{ is sharply peaked for } X\approx \pi^{-1}(x).\]
\[\textbf{K3 (monotone fidelity):}\ \alpha(\lambda)\ \text{decreases with}\ \lambda,\ \ \alpha(\lambda_q)\approx 1.\]
Here \(\pi: \mathrm{MM}\to \mathbb{R}^{3,1}\) is the projection and \(\alpha(\lambda)\) quantifies retention. Retention / coherence law. A convenient invariant parametrization for fidelity is:
\[\alpha(\lambda) = \exp\!\Big[-\big(\tfrac{\lambda}{\lambda_q}\big)^{D_{\mathrm{eff}}}\Big]\]
with \(\lambda_q\) the kernel resolution and \(D_{\mathrm{eff}}\) an effective spectral exponent (controls mode counting). This yields two regimes and a coherence cliff near \(\lambda\approx\lambda_q\). Spectral ladder (lab handle). A light axion-like tower shows non-harmonic spacing governed by \(D_{\mathrm{eff}}\):
\[\frac{m_n}{m_1} = n^{\,1/D_{\mathrm{eff}}},\qquad m_1 \approx 65\,\mathrm{neV}\ \Rightarrow\ f_1 \approx 15.717\,\mathrm{MHz}.\]
This fixes search windows for haloscopes (e.g., DMRadio family) and distinguishes DREAM’s ladder from linear KK spectra. Tensor/torsion channel (space-based band). A torsion/extra-polarization strain in the mHz band can be sketched as
\[ h_T \sim \epsilon_T \, \frac{G\,E}{c^4\,r} \]
with \(\epsilon_T\) an effective coupling, \(E\) a source-scale energy, and \(r\) the distance. This motivates targeted templates for LISA-scale analysis. Falsification gates. Each claim comes with a clean fail: (i) exclude the ALP base window and non-harmonic ratios at forecast sensitivity → ladder claim fails; (ii) no coherence transition near pm scales across diverse controlled platforms → retention law as stated fails; (iii) null results for torsion/extra polarization at modeled levels → that channel fails. The invariants remain fixed; we do not rescue the framework by unconstrained kernel tweaks.

capsule_axioms.title: Key Axioms (capsule)
capsule_axioms.a1: Existence (MM): a compact, orientable, smooth 10D manifold with fields; atemporal in 4D terms.
capsule_axioms.a2: Projection (Kernel): a fixed, non‑invertible finite‑resolution kernel; only its invariants/parameters are measurable.
capsule_axioms.a3: All‑to‑four: mapping is 10→4 (no U4×K6 split required).
capsule_axioms.a4: Constants are projection‑fixed: {c, ℏ, G, …} are kernel parameters in our slice.
capsule_axioms.a5: Topology ↔ classes: particle/gauge classes correspond to MM topological data.
capsule_axioms.a6: Finite resolution ⇒ regimes: below λq quantum‑grade invariants; above classical coarse‑grain.
capsule_axioms.expand:
**A1 — Existence (MM).** A compact, smooth 10D manifold with fields provides the ontic stage; “atemporal” means 4D time emerges under projection rather than being a fundamental MM coordinate. **A2 — Projection (Kernel).** A single fixed, non-invertible kernel effects 10→4 compression at finite resolution. Data constrain only invariants of this kernel class (resolution \(\lambda_q\), locality radius, regularity, effective spectral exponent \(D_{\mathrm{eff}}\)). **A3 — All-to-four.** The mapping does not require a U4×K6 split; all 10 coordinates contribute directly to 4D observables, reducing model-dependent internal factor choices. **A4 — Constants are projection-fixed.** {c, ℏ, G, …} are treated as kernel-fixed parameters in our slice; experiments measure them, not derive them from deeper dynamics. **A5 — Topology ↔ classes.** Stable MM topological data label particle/gauge classes. Individual 4D instances are particular projections of those classes under our kernel. **A6 — Finite resolution ⇒ regimes.** Below \(\lambda_q\): quantum-grade invariants retain; above \(\lambda_q\): classical coarse-grain dominates.
\[\alpha(\lambda)=\exp\!\Big[-\big(\tfrac{\lambda}{\lambda_q}\big)^{D_{\mathrm{eff}}}\Big]\quad\text{(retention)}\]

bets.title: Near‑term bets (crisp numbers)
bets.s1_d: Base mass m₁ ≈ 65 neV → f₁ ≈ 15.717 MHz; non‑harmonic ratios mₙ/m₁ = n^{1/D_eff} (D_eff ≈ 1.72).
bets.s2_d: Fidelity $\\alpha(\\lambda)=\\exp[-(\\lambda/\\lambda_q)^{D_{\\mathrm{eff}}}]$ with $\\lambda_q\\sim 10^{-12}$ m → step‑like loss as the effective probe crosses λ_q.
bets.s3_d: Strain $h_T\\sim \\epsilon_T\\,GE/(c^4 r)$ in the mHz band; optimistic Galactic sources around $\\sim 10^{-21}$.
bets.expand:
**S1 — ALP ladder (lab handle).** A light axion-like tower with base mass \(m_1\approx 65\,\mathrm{neV}\) (\(f_1\approx 15.717\,\mathrm{MHz}\)) and non-harmonic spacing
\[\tfrac{m_n}{m_1}=n^{1/D_{\mathrm{eff}}}\]
distinguishes DREAM from linear KK ladders. Practical windows: tens-of-neV haloscope bands with mode-dependent couplings. **Fail** if the base window and ratios are decisively excluded at forecasted sensitivity. **S2 — Coherence cliff (pm scale).** The fidelity law predicts a sharp retention drop as effective probe scale crosses \(\lambda_q\) (pm regime in several platforms). Interferometric and photonic setups should show a step-like change in contrast/visibility across that boundary.
\[\alpha(\lambda)=\exp[-(\lambda/\lambda_q)^{D_{\mathrm{eff}}}]\]
**Fail** if repeated, controlled scans across the boundary see no transition within error bars. **S3 — Torsion / extra polarization (mHz band).** Large-energy hubs could seed tensor/torsion content detectable by space-based interferometers.
\[ h_T \sim \epsilon_T \, \tfrac{G\,E}{c^4 r} \]
Template families can be targeted; **fail** if null at modeled levels. **Cross-checks.** Cosmological and structural exponents should align with the effective spectral dimension inferred from laboratory windows (\(D_{\mathrm{eff}}\)). Divergences provide leverage for falsification.

why.title: Why this version scores higher
why.text: Projection‑time axiom + kernel guardrails remove ill‑posedness; constants remain axioms. Falsifiability is front‑and‑center with crisp numbers and clear fail gates.

overview.title: Overview
overview.core: DREAM treats the 4D universe as a projection (10→4) of an atemporal 10D Meta‑Manifold. Observables are push‑forwards of 10D fields through a fixed, finite‑resolution kernel. We emphasize testable invariants and a clear separation of science vs. speculation.

spec.title: Speculative perspective
spec.text: Time is a path through pre‑encoded MM states; free will is path selection. Cultural or sentience‑related feedback may influence local projection conditions (interpretive only).

axioms.title: Core Axioms

a1.title: Existence (MM).
a1.text: A compact, orientable, smooth 10D manifold with fields exists; it is atemporal in 4D terms.

a2.title: Projection (Kernel).
a2.text: A fixed, non‑invertible kernel realizes 10→4 projection with finite resolution (parameters are axioms, not derived).

a3.title: All‑to‑four compression.
a3.text: No global split U4×K6 is required; all 10 coordinates contribute to 4D observables.

a4.title: Constants are projection‑fixed.
a4.text: {c, ℏ, G, …} are kernel parameters within our slice; science measures them.

a5.title: Topology ↔ classes.
a5.text: Particle/gauge classes correspond to MM topological data (e.g., π3, H1).

a6.title: Finite resolution ⇒ regimes.
a6.text: Below λq: quantum‑grade invariants; above: classical coarse‑grain.

axioms.note: Axioms intentionally separate what is assumed (kernel parameters) from what is tested (invariants/forecasts).

math.title: Mathematical Frame (minimal)
math.text: The 10D action encodes curvature and fields; projection yields 4D observables. Mathematical guardrails enforce locality, integrability, and spectral well‑posedness.
math.k3: (monotone fidelity): retention decreases with scale, critical λq.

notes.title: Notes
notes.text: “Fractal” is treated as an effective spectral exponent for mode counting (well‑posed surrogate), not a literal non‑integer manifold dimension.

kernel.title: Projection Kernel
kernel.text: Kernel‑agnostic stance: we only commit to invariants (resolution, locality, regularity, effective spectral exponent). A Gaussian is a didactic proxy.
kernel.deff_text: effective spectral dimension enters masses & entropy.

topology.title: Topology → Particle Classes
topology.text: Stable MM topological data label particle/gauge classes (e.g., π3↔integer charge classes; H1↔U(1) sectors). Individual 4D instances are projections of those classes under our kernel.

spec.title: Interpretive notes
spec.text: Index‑theorem links and anomaly safety are required for chiral matter; these are part of the mathematical consistency checklist rather than fixed by DREAM itself.

spectrum.title: Fractal Spectrum & Mass Ladder
spectrum.text1: Effective spectral law on the internal sector yields a non‑linear KK ladder.
spectrum.text2: Thread values used in forecasts: a light ALP around ~65 neV and an exponent consistent with a Deff ≈ 1.7 ±.

imp.title: Implications
imp.i1: Search windows: haloscopes (DMRadio family) around tens of neV.
imp.i2: Couplings may be mode‑dependent; tower could have cut‑offs or suppressions.

pred.title: Testable Predictions
pred.p1: ALP ladder near ~65 neV with non‑linear spacing.
pred.p2: Coherence vs scale: a sharp retention drop near λq (pm‑scale regime) in controlled platforms (e.g., NV interferometry, photonics).
pred.p3: Low‑frequency torsion / gravity tones (LISA band) associated with large energy hubs.
pred.p4: Cosmic structure exponent consistent with predicted spectral/fractal scaling.

how.title: How to falsify
how.h1: Exclude the ALP window at forecasted sensitivity.
how.h2: No coherence cliff near stated λq across platforms.
how.h3: Null LISA/VIRGO/LIGO results against modeled amplitude.
how.h4: Cosmic surveys inconsistent with the scaling.

fals.title: Falsification Protocol
fals.text: Pre‑register forecasts; accept decisive nulls at the forecasted sensitivity. Keep the core kernel‑invariant posture; avoid rescuing hypotheses by unconstrained tweaks.
fals.f1: Mismatch between ALP ladder predictions and haloscope results (given exposure) falsifies the ladder claim.
fals.f2: Absence of a scale‑dependent retention transition falsifies the fidelity law as stated.
fals.f3: Gravitational/torsion anomalies excluded at modeled levels falsify the associated predictions.

faq.title: FAQ — as “Clouds”
faq.note: Tap a cloud to reveal the answer. These entries clearly separate core statements from speculative interpretations.

q1.a: No. In DREAM, 4D observables are push‑forwards of 10D fields via a fixed kernel; constants are kernel‑fixed, not derived.

q2.a: The kernel’s resolution: below it, quantum‑grade invariants retain; above it, coarse‑graining yields classical behavior.

q3.a: A light ALP mode (tens of neV), a coherence‑vs‑scale cliff near pm scales, and possible low‑freq torsion tones (LISA).

q4.a: “Fractal” is implemented as an effective spectral exponent in mode counting; math stays on smooth surrogates.

q5.a: Extreme MM wells may project as 4D black holes; depleted regions as voids. These interpretations are not required for testing kernel invariants.

q6.a: A “resonant loop” between complex observers and the kernel might exist; free will is framed as path selection among pre‑encoded MM branches.

q7.a: Only up to an equivalence class via observable correlators; we identify invariants (resolution, locality, regularity, spectral exponent).

about.title: About this site
about.text: This is a free, Flask‑based multipage site for the DREAM framework. It mirrors the structure of the scientific write‑ups and keeps a clean line between core science and interpretive extensions.

kernel.alt: Illustration: toy projection kernel weaving 10D structure into a 4D slice
kernel.cap: Toy illustration: a finite‑resolution kernel (λq) filters and “weaves” high‑dimensional structure into a 4D projection; grid suggests retained organization.
kernel.cap_short: Toy kernel weave: λq‑limited projection into 4D.
